master_row.title: Sound as the Bridge
master_row.canonical_statement: Chapter: Sound as the Bridge: The Magneto Acoustic Lagrangian and the Dimensional Frequency Ladder Primary Registry IDs: 017–019 (Trawinistic connection, Laplacian, d'Alembertian — the wave operators that govern both acoustic and electromagnetic propagation) Secondary IDs: 002, 020, 025 (lemniscatic topology — the helicity conservation theorem is a manifestation of topological charge conservation) Tertiary IDs: 852–853 (cymatic field evolution and Lyapunov stability — the acoustic modes are a physical realisation of the cymatic field) is the leading order approximation (in $v/c$) of the Trawinistic d'Alembertian (ID 019) applied to the acoustic potential in a rotating frame:

master_row.encyclopedia: sional Frequency Ladder* **Primary Registry IDs:** 017–019 (Trawinistic connection, Laplacian, d'Alembertian — the wave operators that govern both acoustic and electromagnetic propagation) **Secondary IDs:** 002, 020, 025 (lemniscatic topology — the helicity conservation theorem is a manifestation of topological charge conservation) **Tertiary IDs:** 852–853 (cymatic field evolution and Lyapunov stability — the acoustic modes are a physical realisation of the cymatic field) --- ## The Trawinistic d'Alembertian and the Acoustic Wave Equation The acoustic Coriolis equation in the chapter: is the leading-order approximation (in $v/c$) of the Trawinistic d'Alembertian (ID 019) applied to the acoustic potential in a rotating frame: The metric $g_{\mu\nu}$ of the rotating frame (the frame-dragging metric) is the physical realisation of the Trawinistic metric degeneracy — in the rotating frame,
